title: acer tonkinense subsp. kwangsiense
intro: Acer tonkinense subsp. kwangsiense, also known as the Chinese paperbark maple, is a deciduous tree species belonging to the family Sapindaceae. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Acer tonkinense subsp. kwangsiense include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues, as well as its potential role in preventing cancer and cardiovascular diseases. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of the tree are often dried and then used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions to harness its medicinal properties.
